title: sticherus orthocladus
intro: Sticherus orthocladus, also known as the Stinging Club Moss, is a type of plant that belongs to the lycopodiophyte family, characterized by its needle-like leaves and the ability to cause skin irritation due to its spiny scales. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sticherus orthocladus include its potential to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
